findings.problem: The labor-yard opener overproves Delroy's exclusion through four closely related hire-and-reject beats. Each refusal adds local color, but the scene keeps returning to the same story position: Delroy pitches, gets read as old or mismatched, absorbs the sting, and the chapter waits again. That repetition softens the escalation before Claude enters and costs scene pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the opening around fewer, more consequential refusal beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:53, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:79. Keep the racial and linguistic sorting, but either cut or merge at least one contractor pass so each remaining encounter changes Delroy's leverage in a distinct way: one establishes the yard's sorting rules, one exposes the shoulder publicly, and one turns the morning toward Claude's hold before the Claude mention at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:95.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised labor-yard sequence from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:17 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:113, a reviewer can identify a different narrative consequence in each hiring encounter; no two consecutive contractor beats may end at the same outcome of 'Delroy is rejected and nothing else changes.'

findings.problem: Several transient rejectors in the labor lot speak in the same neat, quotable put-down rhythm. The pool-service passenger, landscaper, cleanout driver, and appliance customer all exist to cut Delroy down, and their voices are distinguished more by tags and surface details than by syntax, pressure, or social register. That flattens idiolect separation in a scene that depends on Miami's different hierarchies sounding different.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the lot speakers at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:83 by role and pressure. Keep Delroy's high-contraction, joke-first voice consistent with the style bible, but stop giving each opposing speaker the same polished insult shape. Let one refusal be clipped and transactional, one faux-polite and practical, one mocking or crude, and let at least one line show language friction or broken syntax rather than authorially clean repartee.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised lot scene, if dialogue tags are removed from one refusal line anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:13, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:37, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, a reader can still distinguish at least three different speakers by diction and rhythm alone; no two of those lines should read like interchangeable polished put-downs.

findings.problem: Delroy's interior pressure repeatedly arrives through the same anaphoric mental-verb construction: he tells himself, pictures, or thinks of consequences in stacked lists. Used this many times in one chapter, the pattern becomes audible as a sentence engine rather than fresh thought, flattening the prose just when the chapter needs volatility.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the interiority passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:75, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:179, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:227, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:273. Keep the same family and money pressures, but recast at least two of those beats through concurrent bodily action, interrupted self-correction, or sensory contradiction instead of repeated sentence openings with a mental verb.
findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised chapter, no more than two pressure passages may rely on repeated sentence openings with a mental verb as the main way Delroy processes stress. At least one of the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:179 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:273 must render the money calculus through action or sensation in the moment rather than a stacked memory list.

summary: The chapter clears its causal obligations: Delroy's failed morning, Claude's itemized $400 advance, and the lie he carries home all land cleanly, with no continuity contradiction, tense drift, copied calibration line, or sanitization retreat. Delroy and Marcia both read on-style against the bible. The failure is narrower and technical: the labor-yard opening repeats equivalent rejection beats too long, several transient speakers collapse into a similar cutting dialogue register, and the chapter reuses the same mental-verb/anaphora prose engine often enough to become a tic.
